selected_tasks.body_preview: - Day 134 (09:54) Task 1 (c2da82f3) fixed the case where assessment_missing.md references a nonexistent transcript path — PASS. But the deeper issue remains: the assessment phase sometimes produces no output file at all, and the preseed fallback doesn't detect this gap before handing the implementation agent a reference to a dead or empty assessment. - Day 1
selected_tasks.expected_evidence: Next trajectory shows no planner_no_task sessions caused by assessment-output gaps. Task manifest shows warnings for any stale artifact references. Implementation Notes: The preseed already handles the case where assessment_missing.md exists and references a dead transcript (c2da82f3). Add a check BEFORE that logic: if the assessment file is missing entirely
selected_tasks.title: Detect assessment-output gap in preseed fallback to prevent dead-reference tasks

selected_tasks.body_preview: - Trajectory Day 134: `state_run_unmatched_non_validation_completed_count=35` — the #1 graph-pressure item. 7 of these are tagged as `state_unmatched/open_after_FailureObserved`. - The dashboard at scripts/build_evolution_dashboard.py:2432-2437 already filters recent runs (2-hour cutoff via `_is_recent_run`), so these 35 are genuinely old orphaned runs, not
selected_tasks.expected_evidence: Next trajectory shows `state_run_unmatched_non_validation_completed_count` ≈ 28 (35 minus the 7 closed runs). The `open_after_FailureObserved` tag count drops from 7 to 0. Event file has new RunCompleted events with source="harness" and event_type="error" for the affected run_ids. Implementation Notes: Study the existing scan patterns in append_terminal_stat
selected_tasks.origin: planner (from trajectory graph pressure + #97 revert evidence)
selected_tasks.title: Close open-after-FailureObserved runs in append_terminal_state_events.py
